source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

At Sunrise Robotics, this Forward Deployed Engineer role exists to make our robotic systems work reliably in real factories, and to turn each deployment into something we can repeat and scale.

You’ll own end-to-end delivery in live production environments, working directly with customers to get systems running and meeting real production requirements. At the same time, you’ll identify what slows us down or breaks across deployments, and help turn those insights into improvements in our product, tools, and processes.

You are not just someone who installs machines and leaves. You are the bridge between the field and our development team - the person who has seen the full cycle from design through to live production, and who brings that knowledge back to make us better.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Deploy Sunrise robotic systems in live manufacturing environments and deliver successful customer go-lives

 - Act as the primary technical point of contact for customers during integration and ramp-up

 - Own system performance during deployment, ensuring solutions meet defined operational and reliability standards

 - Troubleshoot deployed systems and resolve technical issues to maintain uptime and reliability

 - Investigate operational incidents and implement fixes that prevent recurrence

 - Support pilot launches and early-stage deployments, ensuring systems reach production readiness

 - Translate real-world production constraints into structured input for product and engineering teams

 - Identify and eliminate sources of non-recurring engineering in deployments

 - Improve deployment workflows, tools, and system architecture to enable repeatable delivery

 - Collaborate with AI, robotics, product, and commercial teams to align customer needs with product evolution


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Strong understanding and hands-on experience programming and deploying industrial robots in real production environments

 - PLC programming experience, ideally with Siemens TIA Portal

 - Experience with simulation environments

 - Ability to read and make changes to electrical wiring diagrams

 - Ability to troubleshoot mechanical and electrical issues on-site

 - Familiarity with industrial communication protocols such as Profinet, Profisafe, and Modbus

 - Experience with industrial safety systems - radars, LiDARs, safety doors

 - Experience with ROS 2

 - Ability to analyse complex systems and generalise solutions across different deployments

 - Experience collaborating with multidisciplinary teams across AI, software, mechanical, and electrical engineering

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to customers and internal teams

 - Ability to work autonomously and take full ownership in the field

 - Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage travel, customers, and delivery simultaneously


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Working knowledge of simulation environments (e.g., NVIDIA Isaac Sim / Omniverse), synthetic data generation, and structured QA processes

 - Familiarity with applied m

source_jobs.title: Open Application
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

Our team works at the intersection of hardware, software, autonomy, and operations to deploy robots that solve meaningful, practical problems. We move quickly, take ownership seriously, and believe the best ideas can come from anywhere.

We know great people don’t always fit neatly into predefined job descriptions. If you’re excited about what we’re building but don’t see a clear match on our careers page, we’d still like to hear from you.


WHO SHOULD APPLY:

This is for builders, operators, and thinkers who believe they can meaningfully contribute to Sunrise Robotics. Your background might be in robotics, AI, hardware, manufacturing, field operations, product, supply chain, recruiting, finance or something entirely different that gives you leverage in our environment.


YOU MIGHT THRIVE HERE IF YOU:

 - Take initiative and prefer ownership over waiting for direction

 - Are comfortable operating in ambiguity

 - Care deeply about quality, speed, and real-world impact

 - Enjoy working across technical and non-technical teams

 - Want to help scale both a product and a company


WHAT YOU MIGHT WORK ON:

That depends on you.

Some roles are predefined. Others emerge from the strengths of the people who join us.

If we move forward, we will define scope around the impact you can create - not force you into a prewritten job description.

We are interested in people who can identify leverage, take ownership, and materially improve how we build, deploy, and operate.


WHY SUNRISE ROBOTICS:

 - We are building in a high-growth environment where the company will not look the same in 12-24 months. That means real ownership. Real responsibility. Real impact.

 - You won’t be joining a fully mapped org chart. You’ll help shape it.

 - We care about talent density, speed of execution, and long-term leverage. If you join Sunrise, you will be trusted with meaningful scope and expected to raise the bar.

 - If you’re motivated by deploying real systems in the real world and want to help build something that lasts - Sunrise is the place to do it.

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure & Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

At Sunrise Robotics, this role ensures that our robots can be deployed, updated and supported reliably at scale. As deployments grow, we need consistent environments, secure access control and automated workflows that remove friction from engineering and reduce operational risk.

Through deep troubleshooting, incident investigation, clean documentation, and clear communication, you will keep our internal systems and engineering infrastructure reliable. You’ll work with Linear as the ticketing/triage system and partner closely with Engineering to eliminate recurring infrastructure problems.

You will own deployment environments, cloud provisioning, endpoint configuration, networking and security practices end to end. Your accountability is straightforward: ensure our infrastructure is consistent, secure and reliable so the team can ship and deploy robots without unnecessary blockers.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Automate and maintain deployment workflows to streamline robot cell rollouts, reduce manual setup, and eliminate recurring operational issues.

 - Containerise and stabilise our robotics software stack to ensure consistent, reproducible environments across development, cloud and deployed production systems.

 - Standardise and manage Linux and Windows environments, establishing repeatable configuration and provisioning practices.

 - Provision and maintain cloud infrastructure, including secure access, permissions and identity management (SSO, SSH), with clear ownership of reliability and recovery practices.

 - Configure and manage networking infrastructure, including VPNs and secure remote access.

 - Own endpoint security and system hardening across internal machines and deployed systems.

 - Design and operate monitoring and alerting systems, leading incident response, root cause analysis and preventative improvements.

 - Own operational intake and prioritisation of infrastructure issues, ensuring clear communication and structured resolution.

 - Maintain clear documentation of environments, incident learnings, processes and recovery procedures to ensure continuity.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Proven experience automating infrastructure and deployment workflows using scripting and programming (e.g. Ansible, Python, Bash).

 - Strong hands-on Linux systems administration experience.

 - Experience with scalable containerised environments in production.

 - Practical experience with cloud infrastructure provisioning, reliability and access management (AWS, GCP or Azure).

 - Solid understanding of networking fundamentals, VPN configuration and secure remote access.

 - Experience implementing and managing credentials, SSO and identity/access control systems.

 - Demonstrated experience configuring, standardising and hardening Linux and Windows endpoints.

 - Experience building monitoring and alerting systems, and using logs and metrics for structured diagnosis.

 - Experience leading incident response, including clear communication, root cause analysis and preventative follow-up actions.

 - Experience establishing repeatable con

source_jobs.title: AI Lead
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

As AI Lead at Sunrise Robotics, you will own the direction, performance, and output of our AI team, building the systems that power robotic deployments in real production environments.

You’ll lead a multidisciplinary team across computer vision, deep learning, and robotics, setting technical direction and ensuring execution against our AI roadmap. This is a hands-on leadership role: you’ll make key architectural decisions, guide the team’s work, and stay close enough to the technical details to ensure what we build performs reliably on real hardware.

Your responsibility is not just to develop models, but to build a high-performing team and deliver AI systems that work in production - from data and training through to deployment and monitoring.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build, lead, and scale a high-performing AI team, including hiring, mentoring, and performance management

 - Set and evolve the technical direction for perception and AI-driven motion control

 - Own execution of the AI roadmap, ensuring delivery against clear priorities and timelines

 - Architect AI systems and infrastructure across training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring

 - Oversee development of end-to-end ML systems running in production environments

 - Ensure high standards of engineering quality, reliability, and operational performance across the team

 - Drive integration of synthetic data, simulation, and real-world data into development workflows

 - Collaborate with robotics, simulation, and deployment teams to bring AI systems into real-world operation


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - Proven experience leading AI or ML teams with clear ownership of delivery and outcomes

 - Strong track record of building and deploying production-grade ML systems

 - Experience hiring, mentoring, and growing high-performing engineering teams

 - Ability to define technical direction and translate it into execution

 - Deep expertise in machine learning or computer vision, including 2D/3D perception systems

 - Strong proficiency in Python, PyTorch, and modern computer vision frameworks

 - Strong understanding of model training, optimisation, deployment, and monitoring

 - Experience with MLOps tooling (e.g. MLflow, W&B, ClearML, or similar)

 - Experience working with real-world data pipelines and production constraints

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to align teams and stakeholders


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Experience leading AI teams in robotics or embodied AI environments

 - Experience with synthetic data generation and domain adaptation

 - Experience deploying and monitoring reinforcement learning systems in production

 - Familiarity with ROS2, Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud platforms (AWS, GCP)

 - Experience working with NVIDIA edge devices or robotics stacks

 - Experience integrating perception systems with real-world hardware


WHY US:

We’re building a new category of intelligent, flexible robotic automation with real deployments, real customers, and momentum across Europe.

 - High exposure: Work directly with founders and leadersh

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

At Sunrise Robotics, this Senior Full Stack Engineer role builds the web applications and interfaces that connect people to our robotics systems. You will own the development of frontend-heavy product features that surface robot state, video streams, operational controls, and system data through reliable, intuitive software. Working closely with engineering and product teams, you will translate complex robotics capabilities into clear user experiences while delivering full-stack functionality across React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL. This role is accountable for shipping well-structured, production-quality features, breaking down complex problems into clear implementation plans and PRs, and proactively resolving technical challenges across the stack - from frontend UX to integrations with robotics services and infrastructure.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Design and build high-quality web interfaces using React and Tailwind for applications that interact with robotics systems and operational tooling.

 - Implement full-stack features across Node.js services, PostgreSQL databases, and frontend applications.

 - Translate complex robotics capabilities and system data into clear, usable UX for operators and engineers.

 - Break down product and engineering problems into structured implementation plans, technical tasks, and PRs.

 - Collaborate with engineering and product teams to shape PRDs, feature scopes, and delivery plans.

 - Integrate web applications with robotics and infrastructure systems, including Python services and ROS-based components when required.

 - Troubleshoot and debug issues across the stack, from frontend rendering to backend APIs and system integrations.

 - Communicate technical blockers early and work collaboratively to resolve them.

 - Use AI development tools effectively to accelerate research, debugging, and implementation while maintaining high code quality.

 - Contribute to maintaining a clean, maintainable codebase and strong engineering standards across the web platform.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Proven experience as a Senior Full Stack Engineer building production web applications.

 - Strong frontend development skills with React and experience building responsive, maintainable UI systems.

 - Demonstrated understanding of good UX design and user interaction patterns in complex applications.

 - Experience building and maintaining Node.js backend services and PostgreSQL-backed systems.

 - Ability to break down complex technical work into structured implementation plans and well-scoped pull requests.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to debug issues across frontend, backend, and integration layers.

 - Experience collaborating closely with engineers and product teams to define and execute technical solutions.

 - Comfort navigating unfamiliar technical domains, reading documentation, and integrating external systems.

 - Ability to leverage AI development tools productively to accelerate engineering work while maintaining engineering judgment.

 - Clear communication skills and a proactive app

source_jobs.title: Robotics Application Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


ABOUT THE ROLE:

Sunrise Robotics builds automation that runs in real factories, on real production lines. This role is how that happens.

As a Robotics Application Engineer, you will take our platform's capabilities - perception, motion planning, manipulation - and build the applications that make a robotic cell do something tangible, useful, and production-ready for a customer. You decide how the pieces fit together. You define the test plan. You validate it works under pressure. You sign it off.

The work is technical and hands-on. You will be close to our robotics cells, close to the engineering teams building the platform, and close to the moment a cell goes live.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Build robotic manipulation applications end-to-end - integrating perception, reasoning, and motion planning into a coherent system that fulfils defined customer requirements.

 - Define and execute test plans to verify application performance against customer KPIs; identify edge cases, recovery paths, and exception handling before anything reaches a production environment.

 - Deploy applications to real-world and simulated robotic systems, and own the technical delivery from initial design through to iterative improvement.

 - Perform A/B testing in production - deploy changes to a canary cell, compare performance against the baseline, and roll out with confidence.

 - Set up logging, observability, and alerting to track application health in the field; diagnose and address issues as they arise.

 - Log and track anomalies systematically, feeding findings back to improve robotic behaviours and applications over time.

 - Set up and maintain software for robot manipulators and grippers.

 - Support our Forward Deploy Engineering team before commissioning and when technical issues escalate beyond field resolution.

 - Contribute in steering the overall cell development product roadmap with your experience obtained developing real-world robotic applications.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Hands-on experience implementing robotic manipulation systems in ROS2 across the full stack - perception, reasoning, and motion planning.

 - Strong software engineering skills in Python, with solid knowledge of Docker for building, deploying, and managing robotics software.

 - Experience deploying complex software systems to production and supporting them in the field.

 - Experience developing with behaviour trees, state machines, or similar orchestration components.

 - The ability to design test plans, define edge cases, and verify system performance across multiple KPIs.

 - Strong troubleshooting and debugging skills across systems that span both software and hardware.

 - Clear written and verbal communication - able to explain technical decisions concisely to people across engineering, AI, and customer-facing teams.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Experience with perception, AI deployment, or machine vision systems.

 - Experience working with ABB robots

 - Familiarity with NVIDIA Isaac Sim, Omniverse, or similar simulation platforms.

 - A master's degree in robotics,

source_jobs.title: Automation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

Sunrise Robotics is hiring an Automation Engineer to own the automation and electrical deliverables that take robotic cells from development into reliable, deployable systems. This role exists because the gap between a working prototype and a production-ready cell is largely won or lost at the controls and electrical layer - and we need someone who owns that layer end to end.

In the next 6 months, you'll establish clean, maintainable PLC code across our R&D cells, bring version control discipline to our automation workflows, and ensure electrical plans are complete and build-ready. By month 12, you'll be the person our delivery and deployment teams rely on when a cell needs to be commissioned, a customer issue needs diagnosing, or a Factory Acceptance Test needs to pass. You own the automation and electrical quality of what we ship.

This is a hands-on, cross-functional role based in Ljubljana, working across R&D and Delivery. You'll work directly with mechanical, safety, and software engineering teams - and travel to customer sites to support commissioning and deployment when it counts.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Develop and maintain Siemens PLC software using TIA Portal, writing code that is structured, readable, and built to be handed over.

 - Create and manage electrical schematics in EPLAN Electric P8, ensuring plans are complete, accurate, and aligned with what the build and deployment teams actually need.

 - Own PLC version control workflows - establishing and maintaining the practices that keep automation software traceable and recoverable.

 - Set up communication protocols including IO-Link, Profisafe, and Profinet, and interface with external controllers such as VFDs, remote I/O, and IO-Link devices.

 - Support R&D on automation and hardware architecture decisions for robotic cells, contributing from the controls and electrical perspective early in the design process.

 - Commission and troubleshoot cells during delivery and deployment, including on-site at customer facilities

 - Lead or support Factory Acceptance Tests, ensuring cells meet specification before handoff.

 - Diagnose and resolve customer hardware issues, working closely with Forward Deploy and Operational Support Engineers in the field.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Siemens certifications in PLC programming or TIA Portal.

 - Safety certifications or formal training in functional safety.

 - Knowledge of functional safety standards beyond the minimum required by the role.

 - Familiarity with robot controllers and how they interface with PLC-based control architectures.


WHY US:

We're building a new category of intelligent, flexible robotic automation with real deployments, real customers, and momentum across Europe.

 - High exposure: Work across the full development-to-delivery lifecycle, collaborating directly with mechanical, safety, software, and product teams on systems that reach live production environments.

 - Career acceleration: Build breadth across both R&D and deployment in a fast-moving robotics company - the kind of exposure that take

source_jobs.title: Simulation Lead
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

As Simulation Lead, you'll shape and deliver the simulation foundation that powers our AI training, solution validation and robot behaviour into high-fidelity virtual environments that scale from data collection to manufacturing running 24/7.

This is a hands-on leadership role. You will build and lead a multidisciplinary Simulation team, set the direction for our digital replica, synthetic data generation, and sim-based validation infrastructure. As a mentor and a leader, you will be responsible for hiring and performance management of the simulation team.

Working at the intersection of robotics, physics simulation, computer graphics, AI, and real-world deployment, you'll help shape Sunrise's simulation roadmap and ensure our virtual environments produce data, models, and behaviours that transfer reliably from sim to the customer in the real world.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build, lead, and mentor a high-performing multidisciplinary Simulation team. Own hiring, performance management, and career development across physics, 3D design, robotics, and software engineering.

 - Set and maintain production-level quality standards, ensuring simulations reliably support AI training, robotics behaviour development, and customer production systems running 24/7.

 - Partner with Simulation Tech Leads on technical direction for digital replicas, synthetic data generation, and customer solution simulation. Contribute to PRDs and technical design documents with constructive, critical feedback.

 - Drive the Simulation team's roadmap - define commitments, uphold accountability, monitor progress, surface risks early, and reduce technical debt.

 - Run the day-to-day operational cadence: reviews, standups, retrospectives, and reporting, ensuring work is predictable, visible, and successfully adopted across engineering.


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - A proven track record leading multidisciplinary teams in simulation, robotics, or graphics/games engineering - including hiring, mentoring, and building high-performing, outcome-driven teams.

 - Strong people-leadership instincts: you give direct feedback fast, grow your team rather than carry it, and identify underperformance and capability gaps early.

 - Technical expertise in robotics and physics-based simulation, with the ability to move between engine internals and production delivery.

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals in Python - sufficient to review PRs critically and contribute to high-leverage code when the team needs it.

 - Production-level engineering experience: you have architected and delivered robust, scalable systems that operate reliably in real-world environments.

 - Hands-on experience with simulation platforms such as NVIDIA Isaac Sim / Isaac Lab, PhysX, MuJoCo, Gazebo, or similar, and with OpenUSD scene building.

 - Experience owning team delivery for systems that run in production with real customers.

 - A product-oriented mindset: you balance technical ambition with operational reliability and customer impact.

 - The ability to communicate complex architectural decisions

source_jobs.title: Robotics Integration Specialist
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

Sunrise Robotics is hiring a Robotics Integration Specialist to own the physical build and operational readiness of every robotic cell in our R&D environment. This role exists because the quality of what we ship to customers starts in the lab - and we need someone who takes that personally.

In the next 6 months, you'll establish ownership over our in-office cells: building, wiring, testing, and maintaining them to a standard that lets our robotics and AI engineers move fast without being blocked by hardware. By month 12, you'll be the person who ensures every cell that leaves our facility is prepared, validated, and ready for a live production environment. You own that handoff.

This is not a desk-bound role. You'll spend your time where the work happens - on the bench, beside the cells, and coordinating the lab activity that keeps R&D moving. You'll be the connective tissue between remote engineers who need hands-on support and the physical systems they're building on.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Own the build, wiring, and integration of all robotic cells in the lab - from initial assembly through to a fully functional, documented state.

 - Coordinate in-office and remote work on robot cells, managing the lab calendar and ensuring cells are available and ready when engineers need them.

 - Test and validate new peripherals, sensors, and hardware subsystems before they're relied on in development or customer delivery.

 - Support remote engineers with hands-on diagnostics, testing, and physical tasks they can't execute themselves.

 - Prepare each robotic cell for customer delivery - setting it up, validating its configuration, and ensuring it meets the bar before it ships.

 - Support QA activities and internal quality checks prior to deployment.

 - Maintain tooling, test rigs, safety infrastructure, and workshop resources so the lab is always operational.

 - Document every R&D cell - its configuration, status, and history - so the team always knows what they're working with.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Proven hands-on experience in robotics, automation, hardware development, or a closely related field.

 - Mechanical and electrical capability - comfortable with wiring, integration, and diagnosing hardware issues at the bench.

 - Working knowledge of Python, Linux, and ROS.

 - High degree of organisation and attention to detail, with a proactive approach to keeping systems functional and work unblocked.

 - Ability to manage multiple work streams independently without losing track of what's live, what's in progress, and what's at risk.

 - Clear, effective communication with both local and remote teams - able to translate physical realities into something engineers working remotely can act on.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Experience with control panels, wiring standards, and electrical plans.

 - Familiarity with pneumatics, industrial actuators, or end-of-arm tooling.

 - Background in robotic cell integration or low-volume hardware prototyping.

 - Experience managing lab environments, test stations, or equipment inventories.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

We're building the GTM engine that will take Sunrise across Europe, and this role sits at the centre of it. Your core job is to open doors - getting the right decision-makers at European manufacturers into a first conversation with us.

You'll work directly with our GTM team running outbound across phone, email, and LinkedIn, designing events, and keeping the operational backbone clean. It's a role where you'll move fast, have real autonomy early, and get exposure to the full commercial function - not just one corner of it.

People who do this role well at Sunrise move into sales, customer success, operations, or marketing. The path is yours to shape.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Execute outbound campaigns across phone, email, and LinkedIn - working closely with our growth and GTM team to target the right contacts at the right companies.

 - Develop and test cold calling scripts against a defined ideal customer profile; iterate quickly on what works.

 - Build, monitor, and reply to multi-touch email and LinkedIn sequences.

 - Respond fast to inbound interest - speed matters at this stage.

 - Help plan and run industry events: roundtables, customer dinners, and trade shows.

 - Keep the operational backbone clean - Attio accuracy, Lemlist continuity, and ongoing workflow refinement.


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - At least one year in a B2B growth, SDR, or demand generation role - you've been on the phones and you're not precious about it.

 - High energy and naturally outgoing - as comfortable working a conference floor as running a morning of cold calls.

 - Genuine curiosity about manufacturing, robotics, and the operational challenges our customers face.

 - Adaptable and willing to take on things outside your comfort zone - this is an early-stage company and the role will evolve.

 - Technically sharp enough to pick things up quickly and contribute to process improvement.

 - Care about quality - every touchpoint with a potential customer should be considered and well-executed.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Multilingual - German is particularly advantageous given our target markets.

 - Background in robotics, manufacturing, or industrial technology.


WHY US:

We're at an early, fast-moving stage - building the commercial infrastructure that will take flexible robotic automation across European manufacturing.

 - Real exposure: You'll work directly with founders, customers, and the full commercial team - not in a siloed SDR function.

 - Ownership early: This isn't a role where you wait to be given responsibility. You'll have autonomy from day one and the support to use it well.

 - Multiple paths forward: Sales, customer success, operations, marketing - people who do this role well have genuine options. We'll back whichever direction fits.

 - The problem matters: You'll be selling automation that makes manufacturing more efficient and more human. It's a compelling story to tell, and customers respond to it.

source_jobs.title: Finance Controller
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

This is our first in-house Finance Controller role, reporting directly to the Head of Finance. You'll own the day-to-day accounting function across our Slovenia, UK, and US entities, drive down month-end turnaround, and help build a finance foundation that can scale with the business.

It's a hands-on, high-ownership role with direct visibility into every part of the company. You'll work closely with the Head of Finance and have a direct line to the leadership team - which means your work matters quickly and the people making decisions will see it.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Run month-end close across Slovenia, UK, and US entities, producing accurate, timely management accounts.

 - Own group consolidation, inter-company eliminations, and multi-currency adjustments.

 - Support tax, statutory, and regulatory filings in coordination with local advisers.

 - Define and maintain accounting policies, including fixed assets and lease accounting under IFRS 16.

 - Run and automate the accounts payable function.

 - Support billing and accounts receivable management.

 - Lead the ERP implementation project alongside the Head of Finance - from system selection through to deployment.


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - Solid accounting and financial skills, built across 6-8 years in a hands-on role - ideally with some exposure to hardware or manufacturing businesses.

 - Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach to month-end and reporting processes.

 - Organised and self-directed, comfortable operating in a lean team where you'll need to manage your own priorities.

 - Curious and willing to learn - this is a role that will grow, and so will the finance function around it.

 - Comfortable working in Google Sheets and at least one accounting platform (Xero, Sage, or similar).


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Full or partial accounting qualification (ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent).

 - Prior experience on an ERP implementation - selection, configuration, or go-live.

 - Experience using AI tools to automate or improve finance processes.


WHY US:

We’re building a new category of intelligent, flexible robotic automation with real deployments, real customers, and momentum across Europe.

 - Real ownership: You'll run the accounting infrastructure across three entities from day one, not pick up someone else's backlog.

 - A project to lead: The ERP implementation is yours to drive - from choosing the right system to getting it live.

 - Direct access: You'll work closely with the Head of Finance and have visibility into every part of a business growing quickly across Europe.

 - Room to grow: The finance function will scale, and so will this role. If you want to take on more than your title suggests, you'll have the space to do it.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

Sunrise Robotics is hiring its first Product Designer. You’d work on Cell Experience (CX), the discipline of making a robotic cell legible, trusted, and integrated by the people working around it and alongside it.

This isn't purely a technical role, as a cell doesn't exist in isolation. It's introduced, explained, handed over, and evaluated by real people: operators on the floor, supervisors managing throughput, plant managers assessing ROI, and the field engineers who commission and maintain them. Design touches all of it: how a cell communicates its state, how a new operator builds confidence with it, how a plant manager understands its value. We're building automation as a service, and that means the experience extends well beyond the screen.

In the first six months, you'll build Sunrise's design language and bring it to life across every CX surface: from operator software interfaces to the physical signals a cell sends across a production floor. A year from now, that language will be coherent across the fleet and across the journey: a person should be able to walk up to any Sunrise cell, in any facility, and operate it safely and confidently without prior training. Your design is what gets us there.

This is a founding design role. You'll define the tools, standards, and quality of craft, working directly with robotics, AI, software, and mechanical teams to make complex, autonomous systems feel predictable and trustworthy in live environments.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Own the design of Cell Experience across every interaction surface: operator software (operations, analytics, settings), physical interfaces (e-stop, lights, sound, feedback), and the service touch-points around them.

 - Design for the full journey: how a cell is introduced, onboarded, monitored, handed over, and understood: by operators, supervisors, plant managers, and field engineers.

 - Define the interaction models, system behaviours, and design principles that make autonomous systems feel safe and intuitive to people who didn't choose them and weren't trained on them.

 - Develop Sunrise's design language into a fleet-coherent system that scales across diverse manufacturing environments.

 - Design for real-world conditions: edge cases, failure modes, alert states, and recovery flows in noisy, high-stakes settings.

 - Prototype concepts across software and physical surfaces: UI flows, light system logic, display hierarchies, and feedback mechanisms.

 - Work directly with engineering disciplines to ensure designs translate faithfully into production-quality behaviour.

 - Validate designs with operators and other users in live settings, and iterate on what actually happens on the floor.

 - Set the standard for design quality, clarity, and craft as the design function grows.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Experience carrying design across both product and service - you think about the full journey, not just the interface.

 - Strong foundation in UX, interaction design, and service design, with high visual quality and the ability to work across all dimensions.

source_jobs.title: AI Deploy Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.


THE ROLE:

Sunrise Robotics builds automation that runs in real factories, on real production lines. As an AI Delivery Engineer, you are the bridge between development and deployment - the person who takes AI vision and robotics applications and makes them work in production, 24/7, on real hardware at customer sites.

You will build and ship applications powered by our internal stack, monitor their performance in the field, and feed what you learn back into improving the platform. Your work has a direct line to deployed robot cells - and your experience in the field will shape how our stack evolves.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build and deploy end-to-end AI vision and robotics applications to production on real-world and simulated robotic systems.

 - Own technical delivery from application design through to deployment and iterative improvement.

 - Monitor, diagnose, and address issues in deployed AI and robotics applications.

 - Configure logging, observability, and alerting systems to track application health and behaviour in the field.

 - Monitor and re-train algorithms in production; perform A/B testing and re-deploy with confidence.

 - Generate and curate data to improve models and applications over time.

 - Work closely with engineering and AI teams to integrate components and deliver production-grade solutions.

 - Support our Forward Deploy Engineering team when technical issues require deeper investigation.


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - A strong background in 2D/3D computer vision and deep learning, with at least 2 years building solutions for production-grade systems.

 - Strong Python skills and hands-on experience with deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow, and computer vision frameworks such as OpenCV.

 - Experience training and deploying AI models, with familiarity with MLOps practices and Docker.

 - Experience with ROS or ROS2 and familiarity with robotics systems.

 - Experience with imaging hardware - industrial cameras, 3D cameras, or stereo systems.

 - The ability to break down complex problems into structured, efficient solutions and communicate them clearly across technical and non-technical teams.

 - Comfortable working with international, cross-functional teams and happy to travel regularly.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT:

 - Experience with synthetic data generation, domain transfer, or designing data collection strategies.

 - Familiarity with AWS or Google Cloud.

 - Experience with NVIDIA edge devices and their robotics stack.

 - Experience with ABB robots and their software suite.


WHY US:

We're building flexible robotic automation that runs in live manufacturing environments across Europe - and this role sits at the intersection of AI research and real-world deployment.

 - Production, not prototypes: Your applications will power robot cells running continuously at customer sites. The feedback loop between what you build and what happens in the field is short and direct.

 - Shape the stack: What you learn in the field feeds directly back into how our platform evolves. This isn't a role where deploy

source_jobs.title: Technical Talent Lead
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.



OUR VISION:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.

The Role:

As an Technical Talent Lead at Sunrise, you’ll own hiring for the next stage of Sunrise’s engineering team growth.

You’ll report directly to the CEO, leading full-cycle recruiting across engineering (AI, Simulation, Robotics, Deployment and Infrastructure) while collaborating on putting systems in place that make hiring structured, repeatable, and fast.

This is a hands-on, high-leverage individual contributor role for someone who thrives in fast-moving environments, works directly with founders, and knows how to deliver quality hires without compromising the bar.

What You’ll Do:

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for key roles across engineering (AI, Simulation, Robotics, Deployment and Infrastructure) - from sourcing to closing.

 - Partner with founders and hiring managers to define roles, sharpen requirements, and drive high-quality hiring decisions.

 - Source proactively through outbound, referrals, and niche technical communities.

 - Deliver exceptional candidate experience - clear communication, fast feedback, and mission-driven storytelling.

 - Build scalable hiring systems - scorecards, interviewer calibration, and ATS best practices (Ashby preferred).

 - Track and improve key metrics - time-to-fill, funnel health, and quality of hire; share insights weekly.

 - Lay the groundwork for growth by documenting processes and support the foundation of building a broader talent team.

Who You Are:

 - Experienced technical recruiter who has successfully hired across multiple engineering functions in high-growth deep tech or robotics/AI environments.

 - You like to understand the business inside out and use this knowledge/insight to help sharpen role requirements, find the best people and act as a true gatekeeper of talent across the organziaton.

 - Comfortable sourcing and closing senior technical talent.

 - Structured and analytical - you use data, scorecards, and evidence to drive decisions.

 - Builder mentality - you enjoy creating lightweight systems and improving process quality.

 - Calm, clear communicator who builds trust quickly with candidates and hiring managers.

 - High integrity and ownership - you move fast without cutting corners.

 - Strong attention to detail.

 - Deep curiosity for how high-performance teams are built and a strong connection to Sunrise’s mission.

 - Ability to automate what can be automated, so you can focus on the human part where it matters.

Good to Have:

 - Experience with Ashby or other modern ATS tools and automations.

 - Familiarity with EU/UK and US startup hiring best practices.

Why Sunrise Robotics:

 - High leverage: You’re the first engineering focused Talent Lead hire; every hire you make materially impacts Sunrise's trajectory.

 - Clarity and trust: Small, focused teams, strong execution, and deep ownership.

 - Systemic impact: Build the foundation for a world-class recruiting function.

 - Mission that matters: Help build the team redefining how things are made.

 - Competitive compensation (including equity) and remote-friendly within CET ± 3h.

notes: Robotics and AI-focused company with a strong engineering presence
